nts1.n: MIDNIGHT MARAUDERS W/ MARSHMELLO & SUKI QUASIMODO
nts1.d: Marsha by name and mellow by nature, MarshmeLLo takes you on a musical journey of all her influences plus a little extra. The first show ever broadcast on the station, Marsha continues to find her groove…

A prolific & singular figure in jungle & drum and bass, Dillinja produced a body of work in the 1990s and 2000s that set a high water mark for club production finesse. Even now, most top producers in the genre are sure to have some secret weapon samples titled "Dillinja bass". 
DJ Persuasion and Dev/Null pull together two hours of his finest tracks, including anthems, rarities, well worn favourites and unreleased dubs on this In Focus.

Mosca runs underground club music inspired by tunes from all over: dub, house, techno, grime and jungle, as well as East coast hip hop, South African house, Shaolin soul, indie and post-rock from his youth, first-wave electro, stripped down ragga and dancehall, a little minimal ghetto stuff, and more. On top of that he's racked up some pretty big guest mixes in past shows.
Elena Colombi helps to keep nights weird - expect a diverse range of up-and-at-'em records: from techno to post-punk, through EBM, industrial and -wave…
Omar Hraib presents The Witching Hour; a passage through the realms of the slow, the heavy and the strange.

nts1.n: Perfect Lives w/ Bruno & Daniel Lichtenstein - Ethiopian Pop Special (R)
nts1.d: London Perfect Lives shopkeepers Bruno and Danny share an hour of discoveries each month, Dipping into charming private press releases, cozy dub & dancehall, way out of left-field synth pop & postpunk, among other surprises..

nts1.n: THE EARLY BIRD w/ CARLA DAL FORNO
nts1.d: Singer-songwriter Carla Dal Forno takes over early morning duties from Melbourne with soft folk, indie and alternative selections.

nts1.n: THE NTS BREAKFAST SHOW W/ FLO
nts1.d: Breakfast, in Flo motion - served by Flo Dill, every Monday to Thursday.

nts1.n: Soup To Nuts w/ John Gómez
nts1.d: Collector John Gómez brightens up Mondays with some music from different times and different places.

nts1.n: ALL FRUITS RIPE W/ JACK SAPSED
DROWNED BY LOCALS w/ Cheree True
Jupiter: Ours by Tarisai Ngangura w/ Immanuel Wilkins
Jupiter: Aesop by Ben Okri w/ Kem Kem
Jupiter: Familiar Music by Caleb Azumah Nelson w/ anaiis

nts2.d: Spacemen 3 were an English alternative rock band, formed in 1982 in Rugby, Warwickshire by Peter Kember and Jason Pierce.

nts2.n: United in Flames w/ Mssingno & Malibu (R)
nts2.d:
Malibu is a French electronic musician whose work sails between ambient and ethereal music.
Forever inspired by soft reverbed vocals and melodious chord progressions, Malibu’s music is an immersive nostalgic journey in a sea of synthetic strings and choirs.
Los Angeles native & synth pioneer Benedek blends ambient, boogie, funk & more, once a month from our LA studio.
An auditory exploration with SF native Mishka ranging from synth pop and balearic to softrock and global dance gems. One hour, once a month, with love.
NYC's Blazer Sound System bring the heat once a month.

Francesco Fusaro, the man behind MFZ Records and classical music recording series 19'40'', brings a considered and varied selection of baroque, classical and contemporary music for a full two hours each month.

nts2.n: Death Is Not The End - 78rpm recordings of Persian Awaz & Tar (R)
nts2.d:
Death Is Not The End digs up the oldest of the old, serving up your gospel, blues and folk with a crackle. Hear the dust.
In the picture Qamar-ol-Moluk Vaziri, known as the queen of Persian music.
Cheeky slacker selections from everyone's favourite - Homeshake. Kick back.
LA pianist and beatmaker Kiefer shares his monthly selection of beats and jazz.
integration sounds from the source; an invitation into the shadow.

nts2.n: UNDER SLEEPY MOON W/ YAWNING PORTAL
THE LAVENDER KITE AUDIO RESEARCH HOUR
BO NINGEN w/ Josephine Moriko & Sofftronic

subtle.d: Saraphim (aka RNS & RDubz) are two Essex based Producer/DJ’s currently focused on Dubstep & the 140 spectrum, mixing ethnical and grime influences into their music. You can expect anything from deep and melodic meditative vibes to high energy driven music.
London based DJ who enjoys exploring the darker, bassier corners of Jungle, Breaks, Techno and Drum & Bass, especially when it is laced with a bit of acid.

subtle.n: The Ice Box Sessions with Frigid

subtle.d: Hailing from northern Italy, STANDARD is a collective of artists, DJs and producers that aims at the promotion of bass music: Garage, UK Funky, Breaks, Leftfield Bass, Leftfield Techno, Dubstep, everything in between and beyond. They focus on the Italian Bass scene, showcasing new up-and-coming artists as well as established talents from all over the peninsula.

Oddkut is a producer & DJ hailing from Balakovo City, Russia. He's part of the Russian collective "United Gang" who are a Moscow-based community of local musicians, DJ's and promoters teamed up by their genuine love & interest in UK underground music, "TAЭT VREMYA" art group and the creator of the Moscow parties "Judder".

Kishan is a DJ and Producer currently based in London. Actively involved in the music scene already, he produces and mixes a wide range of genres including UK Garage, Drum & Bass, House, Disco and Afrobeat.

subtle.n: Out of Phase With Sam Ling

subtle.d: Sessions w/ Amy B is an underground multi-genre music show, showcasing the most prolific guests from all corners of the music industry.

Originally linking up in Wellington and playing shows with the Breaking Beats crew, Swampman and Zero Crumbs have reunited – bringing their smooth mixing and flexible selections to East London. 

In their monthly show, the pair explore the deep, dark, and soulful ends of DNB. Expect fresh underground tunes, deep cut classics, and a bit of banter in Kiwi accents.

subtle.n: Dun Roamin w/ Tomi From Brockley

subtle.d:
Midlands born, London based producer and DJ influenced by UK funky, broken techno, garage and early dubstep. 

Has released on Keysound recordings, RKS, eatmybeat, ec2a, Durkle Disco and Dimsum records

dublab.n: rerun: Time Away with Maral: Persian Classical Music Live
dublab.j: Time Away with Maral: Persian Classical Music Live

dublab.n: Gabie Strong and Ian Wellman - Zone Mapping
Echo Park Film Center - Optical Track
Jennifer Herrema and Kurt Midness - The Banana Question

dublab.d: “Dying Songs focuses on melancholic electronic music but isn’t limited to that. Sometimes it gets dark, sometimes I try to mix techno awkwardly, sometimes i get nostalgic… I like stuff that’s dreamy, ghostly, pretty and sad. I’ll try out new productions of my own, as well. I’m keeping it pretty open and we’ll see how it evolves.” – Jimmy

dublab.n: George Jensen and Heavy Hymns - Quietudes
Daddy Differently and Dirty Dave - Things of Life
dublab.d: It’s the simple things isn’t it? The little things of life. Good food, sunshine, no traffic, dark coffee, 72˚ breeze, telephone off the hook and music every Friday morning a la Daddy Differently & Dirty Dave.
Peak Oil records presents ‘tipping point’; a monthly journey through the record collections of brian and brion. Leaving no good genre unturned, enjoy two hours of esoterica, conversation and guests.

dublab.n: Ill Camille x Jesse Fairfax - The Sweeter the Juice
Greg Belson - Divine Chord Gospel Show
